{{Infobox character
| name = Erastes
| titles = {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|captain}}{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|, iron-haired captain}}
| affiliations = {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Damascan army]]}}{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|, [[Valinhall]]}}
| professions = {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Soldier|soldier]], captain}}
| appears_in = Traveler's Gate
| introduced = [[House of Blades (novel)|House of Blades]]
}}

''See also: [[Erastes Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|Erastes is a captain in the [[Damascan army]], characterized by his iron-gray hair, weathered face, and [[steel]]-colored eyes. A lifelong soldier, he embodies the legendary reputation of [[Damascan]] troops, who serve as the faceless instruments of [[Zakareth]]'s will. Though he does not wear the red-and-gold uniform reserved for those under royal command, his appearance and demeanor reflect a man who has spent his entire [[life]] in military service. He is a skilled swordsman, an ability he notes was enhanced by [[Chaka]], and he has been known to take even children seriously. Throughout his service, Erastes had planned to retire following a peacekeeping mission in the north to live with the [[Agnos family]], with whom he shared long-standing friendships. His history includes a complex relationship with [[Simon]]; while Erastes once arrested [[Simon]]'s villagers, he later expressed that he held no grudge regarding the conflict, though he noted that [[Simon]] had cost him his [[life]] and his men. Erastes has faced various hardships, including being captured, stripped of his armor, and bound by a group of boys. Despite such trials, he has maintained a stoic and fearless temperament, often displaying intense anger or hatred rather than fear when confronted.}}

== House of Blades ==
<div class="wv-facts"><div class="wv-facts-head">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">4</span></div><div class="mw-collapsible mw-collapsed wv-facts-body">
<ul class="wv-fact-list">
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Erastes]] is a captain in the [[Damascan army]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c14">''House of Blades'', chapter 14</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|A soldier was positioned behind [[Erastes]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c14" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Erastes]] has gray eyes.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c15">''House of Blades'', chapter 15</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Erastes]] lay with his armor stripped away and his hands and feet bound with ropes.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c15" />}}</li>
</ul>
</div></div>

== The Crimson Vault ==
<div class="wv-facts"><div class="wv-facts-head">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">4</span></div><div class="mw-collapsible mw-collapsed wv-facts-body">
<ul class="wv-fact-list">
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Erastes]] has iron-gray hair and eyes that look like they were carved from [[steel]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c2">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 2</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Erastes]] was sitting next to [[Chaka]] and munching on a red piece of fruit.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c2" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Erastes]] was a member of the [[Damascan army]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c20">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 20</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Erastes]] is an iron-haired captain.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c25">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 25</ref>}}</li>
</ul>
</div></div>

== City of Light ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|An old [[Damascan]] soldier and [[Valinhall]] [[Traveler]], Erastes is characterized by his cold eyes, iron-gray hair, and black chain tattoos on his arm. After declining [[Olissa]]'s request to become a guardian, he was present during the [[Incarnation]]'s attack and ran forward to assist. During the conflict, he fought [[Benson]] in a skeleton's basement while wearing chain mail and wielding a [[Tartarus steel]] [[sword]]. As part of a group tasked with separating enemies, he waited on the far side of a building and addressed [[Leah]] as [[the Queen]]. He eventually pulled the crimson [[Ragnarus blade]] from the body of the [[Incarnation]].}}

<div class="wv-facts"><div class="wv-facts-head">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">14</span></div><div class="mw-collapsible mw-collapsed wv-facts-body">
<ul class="wv-fact-list">
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Olissa]] tried to get [[Erastes]] to be a guardian, but he turned her down.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c8">''City of Light'', chapter 8</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Erastes]] is fighting [[Benson]] in the skeleton's basement.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c14">''City of Light'', chapter 14</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Erastes]] is a [[Damascan]] soldier with iron-gray hair and cold eyes.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c14" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Erastes]] has black chains printed on his arm.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c14" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Erastes]] is a [[Valinhall]] [[Traveler]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c14" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Erastes]] ran forward to help during the [[Incarnation]]'s attack.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c14" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Erastes]] was present during the encounter with the [[Incarnation]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c14" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Erastes]] wears chain mail and wields a [[Tartarus steel]] [[sword]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c24">''City of Light'', chapter 24</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Erastes]] is an old soldier in [[Valinhall]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c25">''City of Light'', chapter 25</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Erastes]] is present and readying his [[sword]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c25" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Erastes]] is part of a group intended to separate enemies.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c26">''City of Light'', chapter 26</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Erastes]] refers to [[Leah]] as [[the Queen]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c27">''City of Light'', chapter 27</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Erastes]] is waiting on the far side of the building.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c27" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Erastes]] pulled the crimson [[Ragnarus blade]] from the [[Incarnation]]'s body.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c27" />}}</li>
</ul>
</div></div>

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|"I even tried to get Erastes to do it, but he turned me down."<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c8" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{Traveler's Gate nav}}

[[Category:Characters]]
[[Category:House of Blades]]
[[Category:The Crimson Vault]]
[[Category:City of Light]]
[[Category:Traveler's Gate]]
[[Category:Traveler's Gate characters]]
[[Category:Damascan army]]
[[Category:Soldiers]]
[[Category:Titled characters]]
[[Category:Travelers]]
